subject: Geography
title: Bir Tawil: Land Nobody Wants
description: A micro-lesson that makes Bir Tawil: Land Nobody Wants usable.

hook.text: Between Egypt and Sudan lies Bir Tawil—a patch of desert claimed by neither country.

buildup.text: Two different border agreements from 1899 and 1902 created overlapping claims.

discovery.text: Each country claims a different line, and neither claims Bir Tawil as a result.

twist.text: Multiple people have traveled there to 'claim' it, including a dad who wanted to make his daughter a princess.

climax.text: It may be the only unclaimed land on Earth—and nobody wants it.

punchline.text: The one place on Earth no country will fight over.

quiz.question: Why is Bir Tawil unclaimed?
options: Two conflicting border treaties leave it claimed by neither country
